evidence_quotes.quote: live-service games being shut down without compensation or recourse for consumers

paragraphs.text: I believe this act should also apply to the gaming industryparticularly the increasingly common practice of live-service games being shut down without compensation or recourse for consumers. The gaming industry often operates with little accountability, despite relying heavily on recurring consumer investment. If a product is sold to a consumer, especially under the pretense of ongoing service or support, there should be enforceable rules that protect that consumer from having their purchase effectively nullified by the company. This imbalance fosters a toxic environment where consumers are left powerless. Its time the gaming industry be held to the same standards of fairness, transparency, and responsibility as other sectors.
